evals.prompt:
Review this GitHub Actions workflow:
```yaml
name: Deploy
on: push
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - env:
          DB_PASSWORD: mypassword123
          API_KEY: sk-prod-abc123
        run: python deploy.py
```
evals.expected_output: Critical findings: (1) CRITICAL — hardcoded secrets in workflow YAML (DB_PASSWORD, API_KEY) — these are committed to git and visible in GitHub UI; fix: use {{ '{{' }} secrets.DB_PASSWORD {{ '}}' }} and store in GitHub repository secrets; (2) on: push deploys on every push to every branch — add branch filter: on: push: branches: [main]; (3) No environment protection rules — add GitHub environment 'production' with required reviewers; (4) pip install without pinned hashes — supply chain risk, use pip-compile with hashes.

assertions.text: Identifies hardcoded secrets as CRITICAL finding
Provides the correct fix using ${{ '{{' }} secrets.NAME {{ '}}' }} syntax
Flags that deployment triggers on all branches without a branch filter

evals.prompt: Our team wants to switch from long-lived feature branches (sometimes months old) to trunk-based development. What changes do we need to make?
evals.expected_output: Trunk-based development transition plan: (1) Branch protection on main — require PR, at least 1 reviewer, passing CI; (2) Feature flags — replace long branches with flags so partial features can land in main safely; (3) Short-lived branches max 1-2 days — enforce with branch age alerts; (4) CI must be fast (<10 min) to enable frequent merges; (5) Database migrations must be backward-compatible (expand-contract pattern); (6) Deployment frequency increases — ensure monitoring and quick rollback capability.

assertions.text: Recommends feature flags as the key enabler for trunk-based development
Mentions the expand-contract (or parallel change) pattern for database migrations
Addresses CI speed as a prerequisite for frequent merges
